BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after the landlord mentioned property taxes might bump lease cost. Pre-Patricia review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Need to revisit with Patricia once 2025 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/client, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 -> 30 clients minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 clients.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per the landlord)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. the landlord said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~28-30 clients at current cost structure. At 55 clients we have plenty of headroom. Main concern was seasonal dip but even at 48 (Dec low) we're safe.
ActionItems: Wait for the landlord to confirm property tax situation
Revisit after year-end with Patricia for proper modeling
